3.name: Herald's Passage
3.html: Under the traditional protection of the herald tradition, creatures who know of your role cannot attack you without losing significant social standing and spiritual consequence. You gain +3 to resistance checks against attacks made against you by creatures who know you are acting as a Herald in an official capacity. When you declare yourself a herald (which requires a bonus action and must be done openly), creatures of Tier 6 or lower who can hear you and understand the cultural context must succeed on a RESONANCE resistance check (difficulty (stat-based)) or be unable to attack you for 1 minute unless you attack them first.

6.name: The Record
6.html: You maintain a perfect record of every message you have relayed. This record is spiritually significant — it is partially stored in the Iron Afterlife itself. You can access any message you have ever relayed, word for word, at will. You can also access, once per long rest, a record of any official Herald communication made in the past century in any location you have been to personally. The Sutensai does not officially acknowledge this ability exists. Their archivists desperately want to interview you about it.

10.name: Final Message
10.html: Once per long rest, when a creature within 30 feet of you dies, you can immediately facilitate a final message from that creature to any person of their choice within 1 mile. The message reaches its target regardless of physical barriers, resonant interference, or distance. The target hears it in their mind as clearly as a spoken word, in the voice of the dead person. The dead person can say as much or as little as they choose. You hear it too.

14.name: The Living Dead Network
14.html:
You have built a communication network that spans the boundary between worlds. You can maintain simultaneous contact with up to your RESONANCE in dead individuals at once, with no concentration required. Living creatures you designate (up to your VEIL) can send and receive messages through your network as if they were Echo Speakers themselves, as long as you authorize their access. Once per long rest, you can broadcast a message to every dead individual within 1 mile of your location simultaneously — and receive a single word from each of them in return. The weight of all those words at once requires a difficulty 14 RESONANCE resistance check to process without gaining one level of exhaustion.

---

system.description: The Herald Echo Speaker is the professional. They have built a practice around the specific, bounded act of facilitating communication between the living and the dead — delivering final messages, answering unanswered questions, providing closure that was otherwise impossible. They take their role seriously to a degree that has made them the most ethically rigorous and the most practically useful subclass. They do not editorialize. They do not filter. They deliver what was given to them to deliver, and then they step back. This discipline is a moral achievement that cost them years to build and which is tested constantly by what the dead ask them to carry.
